title: 🟢 Trees — Dry Run & Recall
oneSentence: Trace Invert Tree, Level Order Traversal, and Lowest Common Ancestor step-by-step.

invertTree.title: Invert Binary Tree
invertTree.description: Given the root of a binary tree, invert the tree, and return its root.
invertTree.story: Mirror. For every node, swap its left and right child.

quiz.q: Why do we use a temp variable?
options: To avoid losing the left child reference when we overwrite it
quiz.explain: Standard swap operation requires a temporary holding variable.

levelOrder.title: Binary Tree Level Order Traversal
levelOrder.description: Given the root of a binary tree, return the level order traversal of its nodes' values. (i.e., from left to right, level by level).
levelOrder.story: Queue it up. Process nodes level by level using a queue.

steps.desc: Process node and add to current level

quiz.q: Why do we use a Queue for BFS?
options: It processes nodes in Last-In-First-Out order
It processes nodes in First-In-First-Out order, preserving level sequence
It uses less memory than a Stack
quiz.explain: A Queue operates FIFO, which ensures nodes are processed level by level in the order they were discovered.

lca.title: Lowest Common Ancestor of a BST
lca.description: Given a binary search tree (BST), find the lowest common ancestor (LCA) node of two given nodes in the BST.
lca.story: Split point. Find where p and q split paths, or one is the ancestor of the other.
code: public TreeNode lowestCommonAncestor(TreeNode root, TreeNode p, TreeNode q) {

steps.desc: Check if both p and q are in the left subtree
Check if both p and q are in the right subtree
Split point found, or one is root
Return the current root as LCA

quiz.q: How does the BST property help find the LCA?
options: It guarantees the tree is balanced
It allows us to decide whether to search left or right by comparing values
It makes the nodes ordered by height
quiz.explain: In a BST, left children are smaller and right children are larger. This lets us easily trace the path down to p and q.
